name: Text Generate Effect
summary: A text component that reveals words sequentially with blur-to-clear transitions using staggered animations.
detailedDescription: Text Generate Effect creates a smooth text reveal animation where words appear one by one with a blur effect that transitions to sharp focus. The component splits a text string into individual words and wraps each in a motion span that animates from blurred (blur(10px)) and transparent to clear and fully opaque. Using Framer Motion's useAnimate hook with stagger timing, each word appears with a 0.2 second delay, creating a flowing sequential reveal. The animation triggers automatically when the component mounts, with configurable duration and optional blur filter. Text displays in a large, bold typography (2xl with generous line-height and tracking) that works well for hero headings or prominent statements. The component supports both light and dark modes with appropriate text colors. This effect is perfect for introducing key messages, value propositions, or any text content where a gradual, cinematic reveal enhances impact and draws reader attention through the progression of words.
purpose: Create cinematic text reveals with sequential word animations
Emphasize important messaging through gradual disclosure
Add visual polish to hero headings and key statements
behavior: Splits text string into word array for individual animation
Animates each word from blurred/transparent to clear/visible
Applies staggered 0.2s delay between word animations
Triggers animation automatically on component mount
Supports optional blur filter toggle and duration control
Uses Framer Motion's useAnimate for coordinated sequencing
visualCharacteristics: Large bold typography (2xl with wide tracking)
Words blur in sequentially with smooth transitions
Dark text on light or light text on dark backgrounds
Subtle fade and blur convergence effect
recommendations: Use where moderate motion and reusable composition improve UX and clarity.
